markdown:
# Paper 1: Productive Skills (Writing) in IB Spanish B


## 1.1 Overview of Paper 1


Paper 1 evaluates your ability to communicate effectively in written Spanish through various text types. This assessment carries significant weight, representing 25% of your final IB Spanish B grade.

<callout type=""></callout>

## 1.2 Duration and Word Count


### Standard Level (SL)


  * Time allocation: 1 hour 15 minutes
  * Word count: 250-400 words

### Higher Level (HL)


  * Time allocation: 1 hour 30 minutes
  * Word count: 450-600 words

<callout type=""></callout>

## 1.3 Format and Tasks


You'll be presented with three writing prompts, from which you must choose one. Each prompt corresponds to different text types, such as:

  * Blog posts
  * Formal letters
  * Articles
  * Personal journals
  * Speeches
  * Reviews

<callout type=""></callout>

## 1.4 Text Types and Their Characteristics


### Blog Posts


  * Informal to semi-formal tone
  * Personal perspective
  * Engaging opening
  * Use of rhetorical questions
  * Direct address to readers

### Formal Letters


  * Clear structure (date, address, greeting, closing)
  * Formal language
  * Appropriate salutations
  * Professional tone
  * Clear purpose

<callout type=""></callout>

## 1.5 Writing Strategies


### Planning (10-15 minutes)


  1. Analyze the prompt carefully
  2. Brainstorm main ideas
  3. Create a basic outline
  4. Select relevant vocabulary and expressions

### Writing (45-60 minutes)


  1. Introduction
  2. Body paragraphs
  3. Conclusion
  4. Text type-specific features

<callout type=""></callout>

  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>
  <callout type=""></callout>

## 1.6 Assessment Criteria


### Language (12 marks)


  * Vocabulary range and accuracy
  * Grammatical structures
  * Spelling and accents

### Message (12 marks)


  * Content relevance
  * Ideas development
  * Coherence and cohesion

### Format (6 marks)


  * Text type conventions
  * Register appropriateness
  * Overall presentation

<callout type=""></callout>

## 1.7 Success Strategies


### Before the Exam


  * Practice different text types regularly
  * Build vocabulary banks for each theme
  * Learn common expressions and connectors
  * Study model answers

### During the Exam


  * Read all options carefully before choosing
  * Plan your response thoroughly
  * Leave time for revision
  * Count your words
